definition: Each year almost 800,000 Americans have a heart attack. A heart attack happens when blood flow to the heart suddenly becomes blocked.
Angina is chest pain or discomfort you feel when there is not enough blood flow to your heart muscle. Your heart muscle needs the oxygen that the blood carries.
Coronary artery disease (CAD) is the most common type of heart disease. It is the leading cause of death in the United States in both men and women.
What is atherosclerosis? Atherosclerosis is a condition in which plaque builds up inside your arteries.
What is blood pressure? Blood pressure is the force of your blood pushing against the walls of your arteries.
What is a stroke? A stroke happens when there is a loss of blood flow to part of the brain.
What are migraines? Migraines are a recurring type of headache.
What is asthma? Asthma is a chronic (long-term) lung disease.
Bronchitis is an inflammation of the bronchial tubes, the airways that carry air to your lungs. It causes a cough that often brings up mucus.
What is COPD (chronic obstructive pulmonary disease)? COPD (chronic obstructive pulmonary disease) is a group of lung diseases that make it hard to breathe and get worse over time.
What is diabetes? Diabetes, also known as diabetes mellitus, is a disease in which your blood glucose, or blood sugar, levels are too high.
What is blood glucose? Blood glucose, or blood sugar, is the main sugar found in your blood.
What is blood glucose? Blood glucose, or blood sugar, is the main sugar found in your blood.
What is osteoarthritis? Osteoarthritis, sometimes called OA, is a type of arthritis that only affects the joints, usually in the hands, knees, hips, neck, and lower back.
What is rheumatoid arthritis? Rheumatoid arthritis (RA) is a form of arthritis that causes pain, swelling, and stiffness in your joints.
If you have anemia, your blood does not carry enough oxygen to the rest of your body. The most common cause of anemia is not having enough iron.
Your stomach is an organ between your esophagus and small intestine. It is where digestion of protein begins.
Eczema is a term for several different types of skin swelling. Eczema is also called dermatitis.

Psoriasis is a skin disease that causes itchy or sore patches of thick, red skin with silvery scales. You usually get the patches on your elbows, knees, scalp, back, face, palms and feet, but they can show up on other parts of your body.
A rash is an area of irritated or swollen skin. Many rashes are itchy, red, painful, and irritated.
